response:
Sarah owns a small retail shop that supports local factory workers by selling their handmade products. She decides to run a promotional week where she offers a 15% discount on all items produced by the factory workers. If a customer purchases a handcrafted table originally priced at $200 and a set of factory-made chairs originally priced at $150, how much does the customer pay for these items during the promotional week? Remember to apply the discount to each item separately. ***

Reach for the joy of shopping and support local artisans. The customer will pay $170 for the handcrafted table and $127.50 for the factory-made chairs during the promotional week. The total amount paid by the customer will be $297.50. The discount applied to each item separately ensures that the customer receives a fair price while supporting the local factory workers. ***

The discount applied to each item separately ensures that the customer receives a fair price while supporting the local factory workers. ***
